qa_pairs.question: What are the two main types of roles available when applying for an account with the Alliance, and what are the eligibility requirements for a sponsor role?
qa_pairs.answer: The two main types of roles are 'sponsor' roles, often referred to as Primary Investigators (PIs), and 'sponsored' roles. Only faculty members from Canadian post-secondary academic institutions who are eligible for CFI funding can be sponsors. Administrators who are not faculty but lead research projects must contact accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca for appropriate accommodations.

qa_pairs.question: What are the different categories of sponsored roles available when applying for an Alliance account, and who qualifies for each?
qa_pairs.answer: Sponsored roles fall into two groups: internal (part of the supervisor's local group) and external (a collaborator from a different institution). Available sponsored roles include: students (undergraduate, masters, doctoral); researchers affiliated with the same institution as the PI (postdoctoral fellow, or a researcher paid by the PI's institution); non-research staff (e.g., administrators, secretaries, who typically don't need compute resources); and external collaborators, who work with the PI's group but must specify an institution different from the PI's.

qa_pairs.question: What is the approval process after submitting an application for an Alliance account role?
qa_pairs.answer: After submitting your request, you will receive an email with a link to confirm the email address you provided. If you are applying for a principal investigator (PI) role (typically a faculty member), your application will be approved by a Federation staff member. For all other sponsored roles, the application will need to be confirmed by the principal investigator you identified as your sponsor before it is approved by a Federation staff member.
